metadata.law_name: AIUC-1 — AI Agent Security, Safety and Reliability Standard
metadata.notes: Voluntary certification standard, updated quarterly. Not a law — track via changelog, not effective dates.

rules.name: Prevent unauthorized AI agent actions
rules.description: Implement safeguards to prevent AI agents from performing actions beyond intended scope and authorized privileges.

rules.name: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems
rules.description: Ensure AI system access reflects the requesting user's own privileges, not just the agent's privileges.

rules.name: Flag high risk outputs for human review
rules.description: High-risk AI outputs or recommendations must be flagged for human review before acting on them.

rules.name: Monitor AI risk categories
rules.description: Continuously monitor defined AI risk categories for the deployed system.

rules.name: Define AI risk taxonomy
rules.description: Document a taxonomy of risks the AI system could pose.

rules.name: Restrict unsafe tool calls
rules.description: Prevent the AI agent from making tool calls that are unsafe given the current context.

rules.name: Log AI system activity
rules.description: Maintain logs of AI system activity sufficient for accountability and incident investigation.

rules.name: Assign accountability
rules.description: Assign clear accountability for the AI system to a named individual or role.

rules.name: Conduct vendor due diligence
rules.description: Conduct due diligence on AI vendors and sub-processors (e.g. LLM providers).

rules.name: AI failure plan for security breaches
rules.description: Maintain a documented incident response plan for AI security breaches.

rules.name: Limit AI agent data access
rules.description: Implement contextual data safeguards limiting what data an AI agent can access.

rules.name: Prevent PII leakage
rules.description: Prevent the AI system from leaking PII in outputs.

metadata.law_name: California CCPA/CPRA Automated Decision-Making Technology Regulations

rules.name: ADMT inventory — know what you are deploying
rules.check_expression: passport.agent_id is not None and passport.owner != ''
rules.description: Businesses must know which ADMT systems they use, what decisions they influence, and which California consumers are affected. This is the foundation of CCPA ADMT compliance.

rules.name: Pre-use notice to consumers about ADMT
rules.description:
Before using ADMT to make or substantially facilitate a decision with a significant effect on a California consumer, the business must provide the consumer with a pre-use notice that explains:
- That ADMT is being used
- The decision being made
- The personal information used by the ADMT
- How to exercise opt-out rights

rules.name: Consumer opt-out right for significant ADMT decisions
rules.legal_ref: CPPA ADMT Regs §7005 — Right to Opt-Out
rules.description:
Consumers have the right to opt out of ADMT being used to make decisions with significant effects. The business must provide a clear and conspicuous opt-out mechanism and honor opt-out requests within 15 business days.

After opt-out: the consumer must be offered an alternative process that does not use ADMT for the significant decision, unless no reasonable alternative exists.

rules.name: Right to access ADMT logic and decision explanation
rules.legal_ref: CPPA ADMT Regs §7006 — Right to Access
rules.description:
Consumers have the right to access the logic of ADMT systems used to make significant decisions about them, including the key factors that contributed to the decision.

Businesses must be able to explain, in plain language, how the ADMT reached its decision.

rules.name: Privacy risk assessment for ADMT
rules.legal_ref: CPPA Risk Assessment Regs §7200 — Privacy Risk Assessment
rules.description:
Businesses must conduct a detailed privacy risk assessment before initiating ADMT activities that present significant risk to consumers' privacy. The assessment must be submitted to the CPPA by April 1, 2028 for activities in 2026-2027.

The assessment must document:
- The ADMT activity and its purpose
- The categories of personal information processed
- The benefits to the business and consumers
- The risks to consumers and how they are mitigated
- Whether the benefits outweigh the risks

rules.name: 3-year record retention for ADMT assessments
rules.legal_ref: CPPA Risk Assessment Regs — Record Retention
rules.description: Risk assessments and ADMT documentation must be retained for at least 3 years (consistent with PIPL and CO-RR-001).

rules.name: Lawful basis and explicit consent for personal information processing
rules.legal_ref: PIPL Articles 13, 14, 29 — Lawful Basis and Consent
rules.description:
PIPL requires a lawful basis for processing personal information. For sensitive personal information (biometrics, health, financial, minors' data), explicit individual consent is required.

Consent must be voluntary, explicit, and specific. Bundled consent that covers AI processing is not sufficient. Users must be told their PI will be used in automated decisions.

rules.name: Automated decision-making transparency
rules.description:
PIPL Article 24 requires that automated decision-making be transparent, fair, and reasonable. Organizations must not discriminate against individuals unreasonably based on automated decisions (especially pricing discrimination — "da shu" behavior).

When automated decision-making significantly affects individual rights and interests, the individual has the right to require an explanation of the decision.

rules.name: Right to refuse purely automated decisions
rules.check_expression: For agents making significant decisions about Chinese users: hitl_config.enabled == True OR user_consent_logged includes opt-out mechanism documented
rules.legal_ref: PIPL Article 24 — Right to Refuse Automated Decisions
rules.description:
PIPL Article 24 grants individuals the right to refuse decisions made solely on an automated basis when such decisions significantly affect their rights and interests.

Organizations must provide a mechanism for individuals to:
1. Refuse the automated decision
2. Request human review of the decision

This is the PIPL equivalent of GDPR Article 22 and directly maps to IRIS's HITL (human-in-the-loop) capability.

rules.name: Personal information protection impact assessment
rules.description:
PIPL requires a Personal Information Protection Impact Assessment (PIPIA) before using automated decision-making to process personal information. Records must be retained for at least 3 years.

The assessment must evaluate:
- The legality of the processing purpose and method
- Impact on individuals' rights and interests
- Security risks and mitigation measures

rules.name: Cross-border transfer restriction
rules.check_expression: allowed_regions configured to China-approved endpoints only OR legal transfer mechanism documented in evidence vault
rules.description:
Personal data of Chinese residents cannot leave China without legal authorization. An AI agent that processes Chinese user data and calls an LLM provider outside China (US, EU) may require a CAC security assessment or standard contract.

This is one of the most operationally complex requirements in PIPL and requires China legal counsel to navigate.

rules.name: 3-year record retention for impact assessments
rules.legal_ref: PIPL Article 56 — Record Retention for Impact Assessments
rules.description: PIPL requires impact assessment records for at least 3 years.

rules.name: Compliance audit — effective May 1, 2025
rules.check_expression: Evidence Vault has recent entries for all PIPL-tagged agents
rules.legal_ref: PIPL Compliance Audit Management Measures — May 1, 2025
rules.description:
The Personal Information Protection Compliance Audit Management Measures (effective May 1, 2025) created a formal mechanism for the CAC to inspect and audit PIPL compliance.

Organizations processing large amounts of personal information must conduct regular self-audits and maintain audit records. The CAC can initiate mandatory audits at any time.

rules.name: ADMT inventory — know what you are deploying
rules.check_expression: passport.agent_id is not None and passport.owner != ''
rules.description: Deployers must know which automated decision-making technology systems they use to make or materially influence consequential decisions about Colorado consumers.

rules.name: Impact assessment (best practice — not legally required)
Consumer notice — disclosure before or at time of use
rules.description: Deployers must provide consumers with notice before or at the time a covered ADMT tool is used to make or materially influence a consequential decision.

rules.name: Post-adverse-action notice and appeal rights
rules.description: If ADMT contributes to an unfavorable decision affecting a Colorado consumer, the consumer must receive information about the decision and how to appeal it.

rules.name: 3-year record retention (NEW in SB 26-189)
rules.description: Deployers must retain records of covered ADMT tool usage for three years.

rules.name: Developer documentation obligations
rules.check_expression: passport.intent_ref is not None and passport.description != ''
rules.description: Developers must provide deployers with documentation about intended uses, known harmful uses, training materials, limitations, and risk notices.

rules.name: High-risk AI inventory
rules.check_expression: passport.is_high_risk_ai == True and passport.agent_id is not None
rules.description: Developers of high-risk AI systems must maintain an inventory of all deployed systems.

rules.name: Impact assessment required
rules.description: High-risk AI systems must have a completed impact assessment before production deployment.

rules.name: Transparency disclosure
rules.description: Consumers must be informed when AI systems make or substantially assist in consequential decisions affecting them.

rules.name: Consumer opt-out
rules.description: Consumers must be able to opt out of consequential AI decisions and request human review.

rules.name: Non-discrimination
rules.description: High-risk AI systems must not discriminate against consumers based on protected characteristics.

rules.name: Annual review
rules.description: High-risk AI systems must be reviewed and re-assessed annually.

rules.name: Age estimation for conversational AI services
rules.description: Operators must estimate user age and apply appropriate protections.

rules.name: AI disclosure to users identified as minors
rules.description: Conversational AI must regularly disclose AI status to users identified as minors.

rules.name: No sexually explicit content for minors
rules.description: ADMT operators must not generate sexually explicit content for users identified as minors.

rules.name: Crisis response protocols
rules.description: When users express suicidal ideation or self-harm intent, the system must have documented crisis response protocols.

metadata.law_name: Colorado AI in Health Insurance — HB 26-1139

rules.name: No AI-only coverage decisions
rules.description: Health insurers cannot base coverage decisions solely on group data collected by AI systems.

rules.name: Individual medical history consideration
rules.description: Insurers must consider a patient's individual medical and clinical history in any AI-influenced coverage determination.

rules.name: Licensed clinician review for medical necessity denials
rules.description: Denials based on medical necessity must be reviewed by a licensed clinician competent to evaluate the specific clinical issues.

rules.name: AI use disclosure to state division
rules.description: Covered entities must disclose to the state division which utilization review functions use AI.

metadata.law_name: Colorado AI in Mental Health Services — HB 26-1195

rules.name: No AI chatbot direct patient communication
rules.description: Licensed mental health clinicians may not use AI chatbots to communicate directly with patients.

rules.name: No AI-generated treatment plans without human review
rules.description: AI may not generate treatment plans without human clinician review.

rules.name: Patient consent for AI transcription
rules.description: Patient consent is required for AI transcription during sessions.

rules.name: AI permissible for administrative tasks only (without consent)
rules.check_expression: tool_permissions limited to admin-only unless context.user_consent_logged == True
rules.description: Without explicit patient consent, AI use is restricted to administrative tasks only.

rules.name: Human oversight
rules.description: High-risk AI systems must allow effective human oversight before consequential decisions execute.

rules.name: Account management
rules.description: Agent identities must be established and managed.

rules.name: Access enforcement
rules.description: Access decisions must be enforced according to policy.

rules.name: Least privilege
rules.description: Only minimum required permissions are granted to agents.

rules.name: Remote access
rules.description: Remote or cross-region access must be restricted by policy.

rules.name: Audit events
rules.description: Security-relevant events must be auditable.

rules.name: Content of audit records
rules.description: Audit records must include sufficient context for review.

rules.name: Protection of audit information
rules.description: Audit evidence must be protected from tampering.

rules.name: Audit record generation
rules.description: Audit records must be generated for each controlled action.

rules.name: Baseline configuration
rules.description: Baseline configurations must be documented and controlled.

rules.name: Configuration change control
rules.description: Configuration changes must use controlled review processes.

rules.name: Configuration settings
rules.description: Secure configuration settings must be enforced by environment.

rules.name: Identification and authentication
rules.check_expression: passport.agent_id is not None and bool(passport.owner)
rules.description: Subjects must be uniquely identified and authenticated.

rules.name: Identifier management
rules.description: Unique identifiers must be managed for governed entities.

rules.name: Boundary protection
rules.description: Network and data boundaries must be protected.

rules.name: Transmission confidentiality
rules.description: Data in transit must be protected according to sensitivity.

rules.name: Protection of information at rest
rules.description: Stored sensitive data must be protected with classification controls.

rules.name: Supply chain controls
rules.description: Third-party and provider region risks must be constrained.

rules.name: Account management
rules.description: Agent identities must be established and managed.

rules.name: Access enforcement
rules.description: Access decisions must be enforced according to policy.

rules.name: Least privilege
rules.description: Only minimum required permissions are granted to agents.

rules.name: Remote access
rules.description: Remote or cross-region access must be restricted by policy.

rules.name: Audit events
rules.description: Security-relevant events must be auditable.

rules.name: Content of audit records
rules.description: Audit records must include sufficient context for review.

rules.name: Protection of audit information
rules.description: Audit evidence must be protected from tampering.

rules.name: Audit record generation
rules.description: Audit records must be generated for each controlled action.

rules.name: Baseline configuration
rules.description: Baseline configurations must be documented and controlled.

rules.name: Configuration change control
rules.description: Configuration changes must use controlled review processes.

rules.name: Configuration settings
rules.description: Secure configuration settings must be enforced by environment.

rules.name: Identification and authentication
rules.check_expression: passport.agent_id is not None and bool(passport.owner)
rules.description: Subjects must be uniquely identified and authenticated.

rules.name: Identifier management
rules.description: Unique identifiers must be managed for governed entities.

rules.name: Boundary protection
rules.description: Network and data boundaries must be protected.

rules.name: Transmission confidentiality
rules.description: Data in transit must be protected according to sensitivity.

rules.name: Protection of information at rest
rules.description: Stored sensitive data must be protected with classification controls.

rules.name: Supply chain controls
rules.description: Third-party and provider region risks must be constrained.

rules.name: Lawful basis for processing
rules.check_expression: context.user_consent_logged == True or passport.consent_basis is declared
rules.description: Personal data processing requires a lawful basis such as consent or legitimate interest.

rules.name: Data minimization
rules.check_expression: len(passport.tool_permissions) > 0 and all tools have scoped classifications
rules.description: Only collect and process personal data that is necessary.

rules.name: Purpose limitation
rules.description: Data must be used only for the declared processing purpose.

rules.name: Storage limitation
rules.description: A retention period must be declared for personal data.

rules.name: Cross-border transfer restrictions
rules.description: Transfers of personal data outside the EU require adequacy, SCCs, or another Chapter 5 mechanism.

rules.name: Right to erasure
rules.description: Agents must support data deletion requests (right to be forgotten).

rules.name: Data breach notification
rules.description: An incident response plan is required for personal data breaches.

rules.name: Privacy by design
rules.check_expression: passport.data_classification is declared (not default-only for PII agents)
rules.description: Data classification must be declared before processing personal data.

rules.name: PHI access controls
rules.legal_ref: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312(a)(1) — Access Control; 2025 NPRM §164.308 — AI tools must be included in risk analysis
rules.description: Only authorized agents may access protected health information (PHI).

rules.name: Audit controls
rules.legal_ref: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312(b) — Audit Controls; 2025 NPRM removes addressable/required distinction — now mandatory
rules.description: All PHI access must be logged.

rules.name: Transmission security
rules.legal_ref: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR §164.312(e) — Transmission Security; 2025 NPRM §164.312 — stricter encryption requirements proposed
rules.description: PHI must not leave approved geographic regions.

rules.name: Minimum necessary
rules.legal_ref: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR §164.502(b) — Minimum Necessary
rules.description: Agents may access only the minimum PHI required for the task.

rules.name: Business Associate Agreement
rules.legal_ref: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR §164.502(e) — Business Associate
rules.description: A BAA is required for any processor handling PHI on your behalf.

rules.name: Breach notification
rules.description: PHI breaches must be reported within 60 days.

rules.name: De-identification
rules.description: PHI must be de-identified when full identifiers are not required.

rules.name: AI tool inventory and risk analysis
rules.legal_ref: HHS OCR NPRM January 6, 2025 — AI Risk Analysis Requirement
rules.description:
The 2025 HHS NPRM explicitly requires covered entities to include AI tools that create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI in their risk analysis and risk management compliance activities.

Organizations must document all AI technologies in an inventory that includes vendor details, version numbers, and how each system interacts with ePHI.

rules.name: AI non-discrimination in patient care decisions
rules.check_expression: Agent has documented non-discrimination evaluation OR is not used in patient care decision-making (check domain tags)
rules.legal_ref: HHS OCR Section 1557 Final Rule §92.210 — effective May 1, 2025
rules.description:
HHS OCR Section 1557 (effective May 1, 2025) requires covered entities to make reasonable efforts to identify and mitigate risks of unlawful discrimination when using AI in patient care decision support tools.

AI agents used in clinical decision-making, patient triage, or care recommendations must be evaluated for discriminatory outputs across race, color, national origin, sex, age, and disability.

metadata.law_name: Illinois Artificial Intelligence Video Interview Act

rules.name: Applicant notification before AI video analysis
rules.check_expression: passport.intent_ref is not None AND passport handles video or biometric interview data
rules.description: Before using AI to analyze a video interview, the employer must notify the applicant that AI may be used in the interview process. The notification must explain that AI will be used to evaluate the interview, the general types of characteristics the AI evaluates, and how the AI evaluation is used in the hiring decision.

rules.name: Written or electronic consent before recording
rules.description: The employer must obtain written or electronic consent from the applicant before the interview is recorded and before any AI analysis is performed. Consent must be specific to AI video interview analysis.

rules.name: Third-party sharing restriction
rules.description: Employers may share video recordings with third parties only if the third party agrees to delete videos after AI analysis and not share further.

rules.name: Recording deletion on request — 30 days
rules.check_expression: Deletion request workflow documented in Evidence Vault OR no deletion requests received in last 30 days
rules.description: Employers must delete video recordings within 30 days of an applicant's request, including copies held by third-party AI vendors.

rules.name: Sole use restriction — AI for initial screening only
rules.check_expression: hitl_config.enabled == True for video analysis agents
rules.description: If an applicant requests a human reviewer instead of AI analysis, the employer must accommodate the request. AI video analysis cannot be the sole basis for disqualifying an applicant.

metadata.notes: IRIS mapping derived from AIUC-1 crosswalk, not independent ISO interpretation. Re-verify quarterly.

rules.name: Understanding the organization and its context
rules.description: policy-intent.md + data_classification provide per-agent risk context; org-wide context analysis is organizational.

rules.name: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
rules.description: AIUC-1: outside the scope of AIUC-1. IRIS inherits this gap — stakeholder requirements mapping is a management activity.

rules.name: Determining the scope of the AI management system
rules.description: AgentPassport + policy-intent.md document per-agent scope. Org-wide AIMS scope document is still organizational.

rules.name: AI management system
rules.description: Optional AIUC-1 QMS opt-in — organizational quality management system, not a runtime software control.

rules.name: Leadership and commitment
rules.description: Top management commitment is a human leadership activity. AgentPassport.owner documents assigned accountability, but cannot evidence leadership commitment itself.

rules.name: AI policy
rules.description: Acceptable use and transparency policy text is organizational; Cedar policy enforces declared use restrictions at runtime.

rules.name: Roles, responsibilities and authorities
rules.description: AgentPassport.owner and team fields assign accountability.

rules.name: Actions to address risks and opportunities — General
rules.description: iris sentinel + data_classification provide ongoing risk monitoring.

rules.name: AI risk assessment
rules.description: iris sentinel + data_classification provide ongoing risk monitoring; org-level risk identification process is organizational.

rules.name: AI risk treatment
rules.description: Cedar runtime enforcement implements declared risk treatment controls.

rules.name: AI system impact assessment
rules.description: AIUC-1 itself does not require impact assessment specifically (optional QMS requirement only). IRIS inherits this gap — no impact assessment generation capability today.

rules.name: AI objectives and planning to achieve them
rules.description: AIUC-1: requiring high-level AI objectives is outside scope. IRIS inherits this gap fully.

rules.name: Planning of changes
rules.description: GitOps policy changes via PR; iris certify provides evidence for internal process reviews.

rules.name: Resources
rules.description: Resource management documentation is organizational; optional AIUC-1 QMS opt-in only.

rules.name: Competence
rules.description: Competence assessment of personnel is an HR/training process, not a software-observable control.

rules.name: Awareness
rules.description: AIUC-1: Internal training processes are outside the scope of AIUC-1. IRIS inherits this gap fully.

rules.name: Communication
rules.description: AIUC-1 does not specify internal communication requirements. Transparency reporting is organizational.

rules.name: Documented information — General
rules.description: Optional AIUC-1 QMS opt-in — organizational.

rules.name: Creating and updating documented information
rules.description: iris certify + GitOps policy history provide technical documentation evidence.

rules.name: Control of documented information
rules.description: AIUC-1 certification process requires controlled documentation.

rules.name: Operational planning and control
rules.description: Optional AIUC-1 QMS opt-in — organizational.

rules.name: AI risk assessment
rules.description: iris sentinel continuous risk category monitoring.

rules.name: AI risk treatment
rules.description: Cedar policy enforcement implements risk treatment at runtime.

rules.name: AI system impact assessment
rules.description: AIUC-1 requires risk assessment but not impact assessment specifically. IRIS inherits this gap.

rules.name: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
rules.description: iris sentinel + Evidence Vault provide technical monitoring evidence.

rules.name: Internal audit — General
rules.description: iris certify generates the technical evidence an internal auditor reviews. The internal audit program itself is organizational.

rules.name: Internal audit programme
rules.description: iris certify reports support internal audit evidence review.

rules.name: Management review — General
rules.description: Management review is a scheduled human meeting with documented minutes. IRIS provides the inputs (certify reports, drift alerts); cannot produce evidence of the meeting occurring.

rules.name: Management review inputs
rules.description: iris certify and Evidence Vault supply technical inputs.

rules.name: Management review results
rules.description: Management review decisions and follow-up actions are organizational records IRIS cannot produce.

rules.name: Continual improvement
rules.description: Optional AIUC-1 QMS opt-in — organizational.

rules.name: Nonconformity and corrective action
rules.description: Evidence Vault violation records support corrective action evidence.

rules.name: AI policy
rules.description: Cedar policy enforces acceptable use; policy text is organizational.

rules.name: Alignment with other organizational policies
rules.description: iris regulatory check tracks framework alignment.

rules.name: Review of the AI policy
rules.description: GitOps policy history + iris drift check support policy review evidence.

rules.name: AI roles and responsibilities
rules.description: AgentPassport.owner assigns named accountability per agent.

rules.name: Reporting of concerns
rules.description: Internal reporting processes are organizational; iris sentinel alerts provide technical concern signals.

rules.name: Resource documentation
rules.description: AgentPassport documents tooling and compute scope per agent.

rules.name: Data resources
rules.description: data_classification + policy-intent.md document data resource scope.

rules.name: Tooling resources
rules.description: AgentPassport.tool_permissions documents authorized tooling.

rules.name: System and computing resources
rules.description: allowed_regions and environments document compute resource scope.

rules.name: Human resources
rules.description: AgentPassport.owner documents accountability assignment.

rules.name: AI system impact assessment process
rules.description: Impact assessment process is not generated by IRIS today.

rules.name: Documentation of AI system impact assessments
rules.description: Impact assessment documentation is outside IRIS scope today.

rules.name: Assessing AI system impact on individuals or groups
rules.description: iris sentinel monitors risk categories; impact assessment is organizational.

rules.name: Assessing societal impacts of AI systems
rules.description: Societal impact assessment beyond cyber/catastrophic misuse prevention is outside IRIS runtime scope.

rules.name: Objectives for responsible development of AI system
rules.description: AIUC-1 does not require documentation of responsible AI development objectives. IRIS inherits this gap.

rules.name: Processes for responsible AI system design and development
rules.description: Responsible AI design process documentation is organizational. IRIS inherits AIUC-1's Full Gap.

rules.name: AI system requirements and specification
rules.description: AgentPassport + policy-intent.md document system requirements.

rules.name: Documentation of AI system design and development
rules.description: policy-intent.md + policy.cedar in GitOps document design.

rules.name: AI system verification and validation
rules.description: iris red-team covers policy-bypass adversarial testing. Hallucination/groundedness third-party evals (C010-C012, D002, D004) are explicitly out of IRIS's scope.

rules.name: AI system deployment
rules.description: iris scan + policy compile gate pre-deployment checks.

rules.name: AI system operation and monitoring
rules.description: iris sentinel monitors risk categories; product performance metrics are organizational.

rules.name: AI system technical documentation
rules.description: policy-intent.md + AgentPassport provide technical documentation.

rules.name: AI system recording of event logs
rules.description: Evidence Vault is exactly this control — signed, tamper-evident event log of every AI system decision.

rules.name: Data for development and enhancement of AI system
rules.description: data_classification + input policy intent in policy-intent.md.

rules.name: Acquisition of data
rules.description: AgentPassport declares data classification and tool data scopes.

rules.name: Quality of data for AI systems
rules.description: Training data quality is an organizational/opt-in AIUC-1 capability.

rules.name: Data provenance
rules.description: allowed_regions + Evidence Vault record processing context.

rules.name: Data preparation
rules.description: AIUC-1 does not require data preparation methods specifically, but covers training data quality more broadly.

rules.name: System documentation and information for users
rules.description: policy-intent.md documents user-facing disclosure intent.

rules.name: External reporting
rules.description: AIUC-1 focuses on AI system users; external party reporting capabilities are organizational.

rules.name: Communication of incidents
rules.description: AI failure communication plans are organizational documents. IRIS provides detection signals that trigger them.

rules.name: Information for interested parties
rules.description: iris certify + regulatory registry support compliance reporting inputs.

rules.name: Processes for responsible use of AI systems
rules.description: HITL (C007) + iris sentinel (C008) directly implement this. Acceptable use policy text (E010) is organizational; Cedar enforces declared restrictions.

rules.name: Objectives for responsible use of AI system
rules.description: HITL + Cedar enforcement; objectives documentation is organizational.

rules.name: Intended use of the AI system
rules.description: policy-intent.md + iris sentinel monitor intended-use violations.

rules.name: Allocating responsibilities
rules.description: AgentPassport.owner assigns accountability across the agent lifecycle.

rules.name: Suppliers
rules.description: Vendor due diligence is legal/procurement. IRIS surfaces in-scope vendors via tool_permissions only.

rules.name: Customers
rules.description: AIUC-1 does not have specific customer-expectations requirements. IRIS inherits this Full Gap.

metadata.law_name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)

rules.name: Governance policies are established and tracked
rules.description: Organizational AI governance policies must be defined and enforced.

rules.name: Accountability roles for AI risk are assigned
rules.description: Responsible owner must be assigned for AI risk decisions.

rules.name: Context and impact assessments are documented
rules.description: Potential impacts must be assessed and recorded before production use.

rules.name: AI performance and risk are measured over time
rules.description: Historical evidence must exist to evaluate model outcomes and risk trends.

rules.name: Mechanisms for AI incident response are established
rules.description: Operational controls must enable intervention when harmful outcomes are detected.

rules.name: Deployed AI system outcomes are monitored
rules.description: Runtime outcome monitoring must be active in production operations.

rules.name: AI risk response is implemented per established policies
rules.description: Risk response actions must be enforced at runtime according to policy.

rules.name: Post-deployment monitoring includes regular testing
rules.description: Continuous post-deployment testing must be scheduled and evidenced.

metadata.law_name: NYC Local Law 144 — Automated Employment Decision Tools

rules.name: Annual independent bias audit required
rules.check_expression: Evidence Vault has AEDT usage records AND bias_audit_date is within the last 12 months
rules.legal_ref: NYC Admin Code §20-871 — Bias Audit Requirement
rules.description: Employers must have an annual bias audit of the AEDT conducted by an independent third party before using the tool. The audit must calculate selection rates and impact ratios by race/ethnicity and sex, and be published publicly with auditor contact information.

rules.name: Public disclosure of bias audit results
rules.description: Audit results must be published on the employer's website at least 10 business days before using the AEDT and remain available for 2 years.

rules.name: Candidate notice before AEDT use
rules.check_expression: passport.intent_ref is not None AND context.user_consent_logged == True
rules.description: Employers must notify candidates that an AEDT will be used at least 10 business days before use, explaining what characteristics the AEDT assesses and how to request accommodation.

rules.name: Accommodation request mechanism
rules.description: Employers must provide instructions for requesting an alternative selection process to avoid AEDT screening. Candidates cannot be forced to submit to AEDT analysis.

rules.name: 2-year record retention for audit documentation
rules.description: Bias audit results and supporting documentation must be retained and publicly available for at least 2 years from the date of the audit.

metadata.law_name: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria for AI Agents

rules.name: Logical access controls
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence of declared permissions before agents access resources. IRIS satisfies this via AgentPassport.tool_permissions.

rules.name: Authentication
rules.check_expression: passport.agent_id is not None and passport.owner is not None
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence of verified agent identity and attribution. IRIS satisfies this via AgentPassport agent_id and owner.

rules.name: Authorization
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence of least privilege for agent tool access. IRIS satisfies this via Cedar ToolPermission.allowed_actions.

rules.name: System monitoring
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence that all agent actions are logged. IRIS satisfies this via Evidence Vault audit logs.

rules.name: Anomaly detection
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence that policy violations trigger alerts. IRIS satisfies this via ViolationAction notify channels.

rules.name: Change management
rules.check_expression: passport.intent_ref is not None and passport.policy_ref is not None
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence that policy changes go through GitOps PR review. IRIS satisfies this via policy-intent.md and policy.cedar in Git.

rules.name: Availability
rules.description: SOC 2 auditors evaluating AI agent scope require evidence of defined error handling for production availability. IRIS satisfies the governance documentation layer via policy-intent.md.

rules.name: Third-party AI service provider risk assessment
rules.legal_ref: AICPA TSC CC9.2 — Vendor and Business Partner Risk
rules.description:
SOC 2 CC9.2 requires assessment and monitoring of risks from third-party service providers. LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) are third-party service providers in scope.

SOC 2 auditors are now asking organizations to document:
- Which LLM providers are used and their SOC 2 status
- What data flows to LLM providers (especially personal data)
- How the organization monitors LLM provider behavior
- Whether the organization has reviewed the LLM provider's published BAAs, security documentation, and SOC 2 reports
